title: brain git refresh <runId> [--export-challenge | --authorization <path>] — --json success output
description: Regeneration of a review-pending run against the current canonical head: replays the run's plan on top of the latest canonical, produces a new agent commit + manifest + supersession record, and returns the run to review-pending. NON-PRIVILEGED: refresh performs NO canonical/trust/erase mutation — it only regenerates a pending proposal the operator must still approve at the review gate — so it needs no OS-presence authorization; it emits a run.refreshed audit event through the normal audit path (engine core refreshRun, plan §4.5). Idempotent: a repeat refresh against the same canonical head returns the existing superseding commit rather than creating another.

newCommit.description: 40-char hex superseding agent commit regenerated against current canonical.

superseded.description: 40-char hex prior agent commit that this refresh supersedes.

baseCommit.description: 40-char hex current canonical head the new commit was rebased onto.

state.description: The run returns to the review gate after refresh.

x-atlas-contract.summary: Broker-authorized regeneration of a review-pending run against current canonical; new commit + supersession record, back to review-pending; idempotent.

flags.description: Caller-supplied idempotency key; a repeat refresh with the same key + request-hash (requestHashScope: runId, superseded commit, current canonical base) returns the existing superseding commit rather than creating another (registry idempotency: key-accepting).

sideEffects: regenerates the run's plan against current canonical head, producing a new agent commit + manifest
writes a supersession record linking the new commit to the prior one
prohibitedEffects: no canonical mutation (does not integrate; only regenerates the agent branch)
never authorized by --yes; never bypasses the broker challenge
never runs without the canonical-integration exclusive lock
